Extracted from test message PID segment field 5 in setup_task.sh line 28; verified via web search that Springfield is the fictional city from The Simpsons TV show, intentionally used for synthetic patient data

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic test data with realistic formatting but fictional patient information and mismatched pharmaceutical references
pi_items.source: Manual analysis: contains real drug names (Amoxicillin), real pharmacy company (Omnicell), but fictional patient location (Springfield, IL from The Simpsons); NDC code 00069-3060 actually maps to Zithromax/azithromycin not Amoxicillin per FDA NDC directory

privileged_info_summary: The preprocessor_message_normalizer task uses synthetic HL7 v2.5.1 test data created in setup_task.sh with the following verified characteristics: Channel named 'Pharmacy_Message_Normalizer' configured as TCP Listener on port 6661 (standard HL7 port), test message at /home/ga/test_message.hl7 contains 7 HL7 pharmacy order segments with Unix LF line endings (3 spaces trailing whitespace per segment) requiring normalization to CR delimiters, output directory is /tmp/normalized_messages. The test message uses fictional patient data (Springfield, IL address from The Simpsons) combined with real healthcare references (Omnicell company, Amoxicillin antibiotic), but contains NDC code mismatches in the synthetic data. All four metadata claims (channel_name, test_message_path, output_dir, listen_port) are verified correct.
